content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč and an integer XXX. Consider a N√óNN \times NN√óN matrix BBB, where Bi,j=Ai+AjB_{i,j} = A_i + A_jBi,j‚Äč=Ai‚Äč+Aj‚Äč for each valid iii and jjj.

You need to find the number of square submatrices of BBB such that the sum of their elements is XXX. Formally, find the number of quartets (x1,y1,x2,y2)(x_1, y_1, x_2, y_2)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,y2‚Äč) such that 1‚Č§x1‚Č§x2‚Č§N1 \le x_1 \le x_2 \le N1‚Č§x1‚Äč‚Č§x2‚Äč‚Č§N, 1‚Č§y1‚Č§y2‚Č§N1 \le y_1 \le y_2 \le N1‚Č§y1‚Äč‚Č§y2‚Äč‚Č§N, x2‚ąíx1=y2‚ąíy1x_2-x_1 = y_2-y_1x2‚Äč‚ąíx1‚Äč=y2‚Äč‚ąíy1‚Äč and ‚ąĎi=x1x2‚ąĎj=y1y2Bi,j=X\sum_{i=x_1}^{x_2}\sum_{j=y_1}^{y_2} B_{i,j} = X‚ąĎi=x1‚Äčx2‚Äč‚Äč‚ąĎj=y1‚Äčy2‚Äč‚ÄčBi,j‚Äč=X.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ‚Č§ 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains two integers N and X. The second line contains N space‚ÄĎseparated integers A1 ‚Ä¶ AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of square submatrices of B whose elements sum to X.

solution.approach:
For a square submatrix of size k (k√ók) the sum of its elements equals k¬∑(Srow+Scol), where Srow is the sum of the k elements of A that form the rows of the submatrix and Scol is the sum of the k elements that form the columns. Hence we need k¬∑(Srow+Scol)=X, i.e. X must be divisible by k and Srow+Scol = X/k.

For a fixed k we only have to consider all subarrays of A of length k. Let all their sums be stored in a frequency map freq. The number of ordered pairs (row‚ÄĎinterval, column‚ÄĎinterval) with sum X/k is ő£_v freq[v]¬∑freq[X/k‚ÄĎv].

Crucially, k can only be a divisor of X. Since X ‚Č§ 10^6, the number of its divisors is at most about 240. Therefore we iterate over all divisors k of X (that are ‚Č§ N), compute the length‚ÄĎk subarray sums in O(N) using prefix sums, fill the frequency map and add the contribution described above. The total complexity per test case is O(N¬∑d) where d is the number of divisors of X (‚Č§ 240), easily fast for the given limits.

All arithmetic uses 64‚ÄĎbit signed integers because subarray sums can be up to k¬∑10^6 (‚Čą10^11).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N ¬∑ D) per test case, where D is the number of divisors of X (D ‚Č§ 240).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the prefix sum array plus O(N) for the frequency map of a single divisor.
